title: Cannot Print
cardText: Diagnose a printer that does not print when a job is sent.
Check printer power, connection, queue status, default printer, paper, ink/toner, driver, and network access.
Separate printer faults from computer, network, and print queue problems.
text: This guide is for printers that do not print after a document is sent.
Start by checking whether the printer is powered on and ready.
Then check the print queue, selected printer, connection type, and printer status.
Escalate driver, network, or hardware faults when basic checks do not resolve the issue.

checkPrinterPower.title: Check printer power
body: Confirm the printer is turned on.
Check for lights, display messages, or startup sounds.
If there is no sign of power, check the power cable and wall outlet.

restorePrinterPower.title: Restore printer power
body: The printer is not receiving power or is not turned on.
Reconnect the power cable, try another outlet, and turn the printer on.
Once the printer powers on, try printing again.

checkPrinterStatus.title: Check printer status screen
body: Look at the printer screen or indicator lights.
Check for messages such as offline, paper jam, out of paper, low ink, replace toner, door open, or error code.
Printer status messages are usually the fastest way to identify a simple blocking fault.

resolvePrinterError.title: Resolve printer error
body: Follow the message shown on the printer.
Load paper, clear jams, close covers, replace ink or toner, or restart the printer as needed.
After clearing the error, wait until the printer returns to Ready.

printerHardwareFault.title: Printer hardware fault likely
body: The printer is showing an error that cannot be cleared through normal checks.
Record the exact error message or code.
Escalate for printer repair, servicing, or replacement.

checkCorrectPrinter.title: Check selected printer
body: When printing, confirm the correct printer is selected.
A document may be sent to an old printer, PDF printer, OneNote printer, or disconnected device.
Select the correct physical printer and try again.

selectCorrectPrinter.title: Select the correct printer
body: The job was being sent to the wrong printer.
Select the correct printer and print again.
Set it as the default printer if it is used regularly.

checkPrintQueue.title: Check print queue
body: Open the print queue for the printer.
Look for stuck, paused, failed, or pending print jobs.
One failed job can block every job behind it.

clearPrintQueue.title: Clear stuck print jobs
body: Cancel stuck or failed print jobs.
Pause and resume the printer if needed.
Restart the printer and try printing a small test document.

restartPrintSpooler.title: Restart print spooler
body: Restart the computer first if this is a home or small office setup.
If comfortable with Windows services, restart the Print Spooler service.
The print spooler controls the print queue and can sometimes lock up.

checkPrinterConnection.title: Identify printer connection type
body: Determine whether the printer connects by USB, Wi-Fi, Ethernet, or shared network printer.
The next checks depend on how the computer communicates with the printer.
Do not reinstall drivers until the connection path is confirmed.

checkUsbConnection.title: Check USB printer connection
body: Confirm the USB cable is firmly connected to the printer and computer.
Try another USB port on the computer.
Avoid USB hubs during testing because they can cause detection problems.

replaceUsbCable.title: Replace or reconnect USB cable
body: A loose, damaged, or unreliable USB cable can prevent printing.
Reconnect the cable directly to the computer or replace it with a known-good cable.
Retest printing after reconnecting.

checkPrinterDetected.title: Check whether printer is detected
body: Open printer settings and confirm the printer appears as installed.
If the printer is missing, the computer may not be detecting it correctly.
Detection problems usually point to USB, driver, or printer firmware issues.

checkNetworkPrinterConnection.title: Check printer network connection
body: Confirm the printer is connected to the same network as the computer.
For Wi-Fi printers, check the printer's wireless status.
For Ethernet printers, check the network cable and link lights.

reconnectPrinterNetwork.title: Reconnect printer to network
body: The printer is not connected to the network.
Reconnect it to Wi-Fi or Ethernet.
Once the printer is back online, try printing again.

checkPrinterIp.title: Check printer IP address
body: Print or view the printer network status page if available.
Check whether the printer has a valid IP address.
If the printer IP address changed, the computer may still be trying to print to the old address.

restartPrinterRouter.title: Restart printer and router
body: Restart the printer.
If multiple network devices are having problems, restart the router as well.
Wait for the printer to reconnect before testing again.

checkPrinterOnlineStatus.title: Check printer online/offline status
body: Open printer settings and check whether the printer is shown as Online or Offline.
If Windows says the printer is offline while the printer itself is ready, the saved printer connection may be stale.
This commonly happens with network printers after an IP address change.

disableUsePrinterOffline.title: Disable offline mode
body: Open the printer queue options.
Make sure Use Printer Offline is not enabled.
Then try printing a test page.

printTestPage.title: Print a test page
body: Print a test page from printer settings.
A test page removes the original document or application from the troubleshooting path.
If the test page prints, the printer and computer connection are working.

documentOrAppIssue.title: Document or application issue likely
body: The printer works when printing a test page.
The original document, application, page size, print settings, or file format may be the problem.
Try printing from another app or save the document as PDF and print the PDF.

checkDriver.title: Check printer driver
body: Check whether the correct printer driver is installed.
Generic drivers may print poorly or fail with certain printer features.
Driver issues are common after Windows updates, printer changes, or network address changes.

installCorrectDriver.title: Install correct printer driver
body: Install the correct driver from the printer manufacturer or trusted source.
Restart the computer if required.
Print a test page after installation.

reinstallPrinter.title: Remove and re-add printer
body: Remove the printer from printer settings.
Restart the computer if needed.
Add the printer again using USB, network discovery, or the printer IP address.

testPrintAgain.title: Test printing again
body: Send a simple one-page test document to the printer.
Avoid complex documents until basic printing is confirmed.
If this works, retry the original document.

finalPrinterEscalation.title: Further printer diagnostics required
body: The printer has power, appears ready, and basic queue, connection, network, and driver checks have not resolved the fault.
Next steps may include firmware updates, testing from another computer, checking router/firewall rules, or factory resetting the printer.
If the printer fails from multiple devices, the printer itself is likely faulty.
